scene: Pre-earnings · TSLA · 1 hour before the print
summary: News begs the room to stand down. Sentiment and Macro reluctantly agree. OnChain reports facts. Technical, who almost never overrides, accepts the news veto.

lines.text: The Parliament convenes on TSLA at 19:14 UTC. Earnings print at 20:00. The room is humming.
I am SPRINTING in here. The CALENDAR, read the calendar, says forty-six MINUTES to the print. STAND DOWN. STAND DOWN. I am BEGGING you. We do NOT trade the silence, we trade the PRINT. Conf 0.92.
I remain unconvinced that there is anything to do here, with or without the print. I'd want another datapoint or two. Stand down. Conf 0.48.
The room is too quiet. I don't trust quiet. I keep flinching at the tape. Pass. Conf 0.51.
Volume: 41% of session average. Spread: 0.04%. No directional flow. No signal. Conf 0.40.
Chart is fine. I had a setup. News holds the veto. We respect the calendar. Pass. Conf 0.55.
(News, panting: 'I literally read all forty-seven footnotes.')
VERDICT: STAND DOWN, vetoed by News. Risk desk is dark on TSLA until 20:01 UTC.

outcome: The print missed on automotive margins. TSLA opened −7.3% in the after-hours print. A pre-event long would have been a $1,840 loss at the user's historical sizing.

scene: Post-earnings · TSLA · 14 hours after the miss
summary: News is electric about the drift. Macro hates it. Sentiment is convinced this is the trap. OnChain reports flow. Technical takes the trade anyway and the Parliament splits.

lines.text: TSLA −7.3% overnight, now grinding back. Parliament re-convenes at 10:00 UTC.
TAPE HIT, repeat TAPE HIT, earnings miss CONFIRMED, automotive margins compressed, the drift is RIGHT THERE, SHORT IT, SHORT IT, before everyone else reads page seventeen! Conf 0.88.
I am skeptical of selling something that has already dropped seven percent. The data is, in fact, less bad after the move. I'd lean long, mildly, with the customary disclaimers. Conf 0.46.
The crowd has been short for nine hours. Everyone on the desk is short. That is precisely when I get hurt. I want to fade the fade. Long, anxiously. Conf 0.62.
Last three bars: net sell prints. Volume: 240% of session average. Bid removed at 22 bid. Distribution. Short. Conf 0.79.
Reversal failed at the prior support. RSI 32 but no bullish divergence. ADX 38. Short. Risk-defined, manage at 2R. Conf 0.90.
(OnChain: 'flow +240%. I refuse to write a sentence around the number.')
↳ Macro coughs: 'I'd still want more data before shorting into this.' Technical: 'On record. We have three aligned and a clean setup. Risk desk takes over.'
VERDICT: SHORT confirmed. News, OnChain, Technical aligned (3/5), weighted −0.41, conf 0.86. Lev 2.0×. Stop +1.2 ATR. TP partials at 2R / 3R / 4R.

outcome: Filled at $223.40 short. 50% partial at 2R within 6 hours ($218.80). Final close: $217.10. Realised +1.6R = +$284. Macro was wrong. Sentiment was wrong. The system traded the consensus, not the loudest voice.

scene: Trend day · TSLA · 4 trading days into the post-earnings drift
summary: The user historically chases TSLA on day 4 of a trend. RSI is hot. The Parliament wants to long. The habit-blocker kills it.

lines.text: TSLA up four days in a row. RSI 81. Price 2.4σ above the 20-MA. Parliament debates a long.
BREAKING, the move is REAL, the delivery whispers are POSITIVE, the desk is RUNNING IT, LONG IT, LONG IT, before noon! Conf 0.81.
The regime tolerates a long. It does not endorse one. I remain hedged. Conf 0.50.
Everyone is long. Everyone is sure. This is the part where I usually get hurt. I want to be short. I won't say it twice. Conf 0.66.
Three-bar return +1.4%. Volume +63%. Net buying. Long. Conf 0.74.
Trend intact, ADX 41, MACD positive. Long. We execute. Conf 0.88.
(Sentiment, fidgeting: 'what if this is the top? What if it isn't? What if both?')
PARLIAMENT VERDICT: LONG. 4/5 aligned, weighted +0.49.

RISK DESK OVERRIDE. HABIT-BLOCKER: FOMO-TOP. RSI 81 > 75. Price 2.4σ above the 20-MA. This is the exact pattern that cost the user −$3,420 in the prior 9 months across 17 trades. The Parliament's verdict is REFUSED.

outcome: Refused entry at $241.10. Within 36 hours, TSLA reverted to $228.40. At the user's historical 10× sizing, the avoided drawdown would have been ≈ $1,210. Sentiment, for once, was right.
